title: Healing Arts 6-Session Workshop
location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Healing Arts 6-Session Workshop
Fridays, 10-11:45 AM | Mar. 13, 20, 27 & Apr. 3, 10, 17
$25 Members, $35 Non-Members (all materials included) 
The Pence’s 6-session Healing Arts workshop uses art as a healing language for those experiencing grief from the loss of a loved one. Through the process of art making, participants will have opportunities for discussion, education, and connecting with peers in a safe and supportive space in the Pence Learning Center under the guidance of facilitators Amy Vidra and Lyndsey Rocca. 
Registration is required in advance through our website, and space is limited to the first 8 participants who register. Participants must be 18 or older, and no art experience is necessary. This program is generously sponsored by Bob & Joanne Andresen. 
Register (REQUIRED): https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/ 
[Please note that when registering for this program, you are signing up for all 6 sessions. If you are unable to attend one or more of the sessions, please let us know in advance.] 
Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Village Homes: A Radical Plan
location: UC Davis Design Museum, Cruess Hall, #124, Davis, 95616, United States
description: “Village Homes: A Radical Plan” celebrates the 50th anniversary of an innovative west Davis neighborhood community. Curated by Professor Timothy McNeil and guest curator Adrienne McGraw, the installation showcases the vision, concepts, and development behind the project and its goals. This ground-breaking exhibition will feature original objects, archival photographs, plans and drawings, a part-built section of a typical 1970s house in Village Homes recreating a living space and demonstrating the passive solar features, and an interactive three-dimensional site plan of the neighborhood’s unique landscape. Other highlights include a 30-minute documentary film about the community.

title: 2nd Friday ArtAbout reception: Futaba Wood & Walker Mason
location: Logos Books, 513 2nd Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Living Together in Harmony
Futaba Wood: watercolor, acrylic
Walker Mason: photography
March 3 to March 28
2nd Friday ArtAbout Reception: March 13, 5:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m.
Logos Books, 513 Second Street, Davis, CA, 95616 
From Futaba Wood:
Life can be chaotic, heartbreaking and enigmatic. Life can also be vibrant, joyous and transformative. It’s far from simple, but there is a certain kind of harmony to it all. That harmony is sometimes most apparent when we realize that, despite our differences, we are all quite similar. In nature, many kinds of animals live side by side. Can humans – divided by race, gender, and background – do the same in this world? Or is this hope merely a fragile dream? These thoughts played an important role in our approach to this show. The two of us come from different backgrounds and generations and we each employ distinct mediums to create our art. In this show we wanted to celebrate our individual perspectives while simultaneously highlighting our shared appreciation for the natural world and the intrinsic harmony of life. Email: dfwood@comcast.net 
From Walker Mason:
I’ve experimented with a number of different art forms, but photography is where my heart truly lies. For me the true beauty of photography is that I can create art everywhere I go. I enjoy capturing the things I encounter in my daily life. While studying at the University of Hawai’i I became enthralled with the darkroom process. My time there reshaped the way I work. Today, I strive to honor the tradition of analog photography and share my passion with others. Instagram @heron_photographic

title: 2nd Friday ArtAbout at the Pence Gallery
location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Join us at the Pence Gallery for our 2nd Friday ArtAbout reception on March 13, 6-9 PM. Enjoy browsing multiple art exhibits, complimentary wine by Matchbook Wines, and an art-themed scavenger hunt. Also, view special floral arrangements within the exhibits by the Davis Flower Arrangers. Free admission! 
Manuel Fernando Rios: When They Reminisce Over You | Feb. 7 – Mar. 29
The artist’s work explores his Chicano heritage, as well as his family’s history and experiences. Blurring the line between naturalism and abstraction, he embraces the use of complex design patterns and photography to make a powerful statement. 
Assorted Flavors: Pence Member Exhibit | Mar. 13 – Apr. 16
Our 2nd annual non-juried exhibit celebrates the creativity and talent of our organization’s supporters, showcasing work in different media by 40 Pence Members. 
Barbara Prodaniuk: Myths in Mud | Mar. 13 – Apr. 26
The ceramic artist’s work is inspired by the forest, meadows, and waterways surrounding the Truckee area. She explores the stories carried by the creatures and artifacts found in these environments through layered surfaces, organic textures, and sculpted imagery. 
Loraine Watson-Fox: Small Wonders | Mar. 6 – Mar. 29
The artist employs mixed-media including collage, text, thread and textural elements to enhance her use of more traditional paint media. Her work is on display along the stairway. 
Davis Flower Arrangers: Art in Bloom | Mar. 13 – Mar. 15
In a special collaboration, the Davis Flower Arrangers have put together creative floral arrangements to be paired with specific artworks on view at the Pence. 
Scavenger Hunt: Join us for a fun, art-themed scavenger hunt at the Pence during the 2nd Friday ArtAbout. Complete all of the clues by exploring our exhibits for the chance to win a special prize. 
— 
Visit the Pence and other venues in Downtown Davis for the 2nd Friday ArtAbout artwalk. We will have printed ArtAbout guides available that week. Follow the ArtAbout’s Facebook page for more information: https://www.facebook.com/davisartabout.

title: Art in Bloom
location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Art in Bloom 
Dates/times: March 13, 6-9 PM, March 14-15, 11:30 AM – 5 PM
Location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA 95616
Admission: Admission to the Pence is free; we welcome donations from visitors, as they help us cover basic costs associated with running a nonprofit art gallery. 
Art in Bloom is the first collaboration with members of the Davis Flower Arrangers and the Pence Gallery, in which creative floral arrangements are paired with specific artworks on view at the Pence from March 13-15. On Friday, March 13, from 6-9 PM, enjoy meeting many of the participating floral designers and see the arrangements as they are at their freshest, as part of the city’s 2nd Friday ArtAbout reception. Visitors are welcome to enjoy a variety of arrangements that include traditional and unusual materials, and a variety of beautiful containers. The connection between the artwork and floral design can include using similar colors, themes, textures, or overall composition. 
Participating floral designers include Janet Goehring, Joyce Hamaguchi, Elly Fairclough, MJ Kelly, Rhody Vallejo, Elisabetta Vivoda, Mike MacDonald, Stephanie DeGraff-Hunt, and Julie Haney. Each artist has chosen an artwork for inspiration, and will create a vibrant interpretation of pieces by exhibiting artists Manuel Fernando Rios, Barbara Prodaniuk, and artists from Assorted Flavors, our annual exhibit of artworks created by forty Pence members. 
Founded in 1958, the Davis Flower Arrangers is a long-standing organization in Davis, dedicated to studying and instructing floral design techniques, including Ikebana. The group, celebrating its 67th season in 2025-26, promotes the public appreciation of floral art through workshops, demonstrations, and community events. For more information on the Davis Flower Arrangers, contact President Stephanie DeGraff-Hunt at sdegraffhunt@gmail.com.

title: Boca do Rio Live at Morgan’s Mill
location: Morgan’s Mill, 1059 Court St., Woodland, CA, 95695-9112, United States
description:
Boca do Rio Live at Morgan’s Mill – Friday, March 13 
Left of the Dial Promotions presents Boca do Rio, live at Morgan’s Mill in Woodland on Friday, March 13 from 6–8 PM. Born as a bossa nova trio in 1999, Boca do Rio has evolved into a high-energy electro-funk ensemble blending traditional Brazilian rhythms with contemporary Bay Area grooves. Based in Davis, the band delivers a vibrant, danceable sound that’s both rooted and forward-thinking. 
This free, all-ages show invites the community to experience a night of rhythmic exploration, soulful melodies, and global fusion in an intimate café setting. 
Venue: Morgan’s Mill – 262 East St, Woodland, CA
Time: 6:00–8:00 PM
Admission: Free | All Ages

title: Choruses of UC Davis: “Music of the Living”
location: Ann E. Pitzer Center, Hutchison Dr & Cushing Way, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
In a program of music written entirely by living composers, the choruses at UC Davis perform choral works ideal for the fine acoustics of the Pitzer Center. The repertoire incorporates several different choral traditions and languages, from Latin, to Haitian Creole, to Korean and Portuguese. The program concludes with “World O World” from the “Djesse” music project by the five-time Grammy Award-winning composer Jacob Collier. 
$12 Students and Children, $24 Adults (Open Seating)

description:
Year-round at Central Park in downtown Davis, thousands gather each week to shop for what is grown, raised, and made locally at our markets. We are dedicated to bringing farmers right to your table, so we stay open rain or shine. 
Walk rows of stalls lined with an array of fruits and vegetables, including certified organic produce, choice meats, chicken, seafood, wine, local eggs and honey. title: Second Saturday Explorer Ride
description:
Here’s a link to the March second Saturday ride: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/54187228

The plan is to go from Davis to Hedgerow Farms to see if any of the flower fields are blooming, ride to the scenic west end of Road 29, stop in Winters for a snack or lunch at Upper Crust, and then head back to Davis.

More details below. Please let me know if you need additional information.
Thanks,
-Anne, (916) 206-2922

Date: Second Saturday – March 14, 2026
Start time: 9:00 a.m.
Start location: Davis: Westlake Shopping Center, 1260 Lake Blvd.
Distance: 45 miles
Contact: Anne Huber; anneh@dcn.org; 916-206-2922
Services (Food, water, bathrooms): in Winters at mile 32, so you might want to bring a snack
No need to RSVP
Rain cancellation or postponement: communicated via the listserv.
Pace: 14 - 17 mph with regroups. The speed is meant to be an indicator of speed on flat terrain with no wind. People can go faster if they want as long as they are willing to regroup. There might be some stopping for scenery.
Route: https://ridewithgps.com/routes/54187228

For general information about the second Saturday ride and bicycling with the Davis Bike Club, please see: https://davisbikeclub.org/2nd-sat-explorer-ride/

It includes the following: “Cycling has inherent risks. Any cyclist taking part in a DBC ride releases DBC from any liability. Guests are welcome to ride with DBC for one ride; for subsequent rides, you must be a member of the Club. By riding with a DBC group ride you acknowledge that you have read and understand this. Helmets are required on all rides"

title: Beetlejuice Jr
location: Davis Musical Theatre Company (DMTC), The Jean Henderson Performing Arts Center, 607 Pena Drive, Davis
description:
Beetlejuice Jr – March 14 through April 5, 2026
School Matinees: March 31, 2026; 9:15am and noon 
  
It’s our show now, kid. The ghost-with-the-most comes to the stage in Beetlejuice JR., the frightfully funny musical based on the beloved motion picture turned Broadway phenomenon. Lydia Deetz is a strange and unusual teenager, still grieving the loss of her mother and obsessed with the whole “being dead thing.” Lucky for Lydia, she and her father move to a new house haunted by a recently deceased couple and Beetlejuice, a delightful demon with a real zest for life. When Lydia calls on Beetlejuice to scare away anyone with a pulse, this double-crossing specter shows his true stripes, unleashing a (Nether)world of pandemonium. 
Recommended for ages 10 & up 
  
Directed by Tea Pusey
Choreographed by Logan Sexton
Musical direction by Montana Monce

Clap your hands, tap your feet, and yell all you want – the Renegade Orchestra embraces the virtuosic skills of top bay area musicians and turns them loose on songs orchestras have never or maybe shouldn’t do.

title: Art in Bloom
location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Art in Bloom 
  
Dates/times: March 13, 6-9 PM, March 14-15, 11:30 AM – 5 PM  
Location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA 95616 
Admission: Admission to the Pence is free; we welcome donations from visitors, as they help us cover basic costs associated with running a nonprofit art gallery. 
  
Art in Bloom is the first collaboration with members of the Davis Flower Arrangers and the Pence Gallery, in which creative floral arrangements are paired with specific artworks on view at the Pence from March 13-15. On Friday, March 13, from 6-9 PM, enjoy meeting many of the participating floral designers and see the arrangements as they are at their freshest, as part of the city’s 2nd Friday ArtAbout reception. Visitors are welcome to enjoy a variety of arrangements that include traditional and unusual materials, and a variety of beautiful containers. The connection between the artwork and floral design can include using similar colors, themes, textures, or overall composition.  
  
Participating floral designers include Janet Goehring, Joyce Hamaguchi, Elly Fairclough, MJ Kelly, Rhody Vallejo, Elisabetta Vivoda, Mike MacDonald, Stephanie DeGraff-Hunt, and Julie Haney. Each artist has chosen an artwork for inspiration, and will create a vibrant interpretation of pieces by exhibiting artists Manuel Fernando Rios, Barbara Prodaniuk, and artists from Assorted Flavors, our annual exhibit of artworks created by forty Pence members.  
  
Founded in 1958, the Davis Flower Arrangers is a long-standing organization in Davis, dedicated to studying and instructing floral design techniques, including Ikebana. The group, celebrating its 67th season in 2025-26, promotes the public appreciation of floral art through workshops, demonstrations, and community events. For more information on the Davis Flower Arrangers, contact President Stephanie DeGraff-Hunt at sdegraffhunt@gmail.com.  
  
Image: Davis Flower Arrangement (past event)

title: Intuitive Soul Stories: Oracle Card Making Workshop
description:
Iama Embodied Community
Unlock the stories and symbols that live within you in this gentle, intuitive collage workshop with guest facilitator Rachel Hodge.
This isn’t about being an “artist”—it’s about curiosity, intuition, and self-discovery. Using simple images and collage, you’ll create a personal deck of oracle-style cards that reflect different parts of your inner world. Each card becomes a “soul companion,” offering clarity, grounding, and a direct line to your own innate wisdom.
No artistic experience is needed—just bring your curiosity and openness. Materials will be provided, and the space is relaxed, reflective, and supportive.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:

**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults - Virtual
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.

title: Mental Health First Aid Training
location: Yolo Couty Health and Human Services, 25 N Cottonwood St, Woodland, CA, 95695, United States
description:
This free training is open to Yolo County staff, provider staff and the community. Mental Health First Aid teaches you how to identify, understand and respond to signs of mental health and substance use challenges. The training will also cover how to interact with a person in crisis and connect the person with help. 
  
Attendees must complete a two-hour virtual training prior to the class and are expected to attend the live training for the full duration. After successfully passing the prework, attendees will receive a three-year professional certification on Mental Health First Aid. 
  
To register for this free training, e-mail your contact information to: MHSA-MHTrainings@yolocounty.org. Questions? Call 530-666-8710

title: Gel Printing Workshop with Sara Post
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Under the guidance of artist Sara Post, students will explore the ins and out of gel printing. Participants will experiment with layering, masks, stencils, and other gel printing techniques, as well as work with a variety of papers. Gel prints, beautiful in themselves, are also a wonderful way to make handmade papers for collage or under layers for painting. Students will leave this class with a portfolio of prints to display on their own or to use in making other artworks.

title: Beetlejuice Jr
location: Davis Musical Theatre Company (DMTC), The Jean Henderson Performing Arts Center, 607 Pena Drive, Davis
description:
Beetlejuice Jr – March 14 through April 5, 2026
School Matinees: March 31, 2026; 9:15am and noon 
  
It’s our show now, kid. The ghost-with-the-most comes to the stage in Beetlejuice JR., the frightfully funny musical based on the beloved motion picture turned Broadway phenomenon. Lydia Deetz is a strange and unusual teenager, still grieving the loss of her mother and obsessed with the whole “being dead thing.” Lucky for Lydia, she and her father move to a new house haunted by a recently deceased couple and Beetlejuice, a delightful demon with a real zest for life. When Lydia calls on Beetlejuice to scare away anyone with a pulse, this double-crossing specter shows his true stripes, unleashing a (Nether)world of pandemonium. 
Recommended for ages 10 & up 
  
Directed by Tea Pusey
Choreographed by Logan Sexton
Musical direction by Montana Monce

title: Intermediate Watercolor Painting with Misuk Goltz (6-Sessions)
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
In this 6-week workshop, students will create watercolor paintings inspired by Misuk’s recent travels to Italy, Croatia, Montenegro, Greece, and Turkey. Using these Mediterranean image references, participants will explore landscape, architectural, and figurative subjects rich in light, atmosphere and color. Students will focus on designing harmonious compositions, developing value studies for each subject, and working with limited color palettes to achieve cohesive, painterly results. For this workshop, participants should have prior experience with watercolor techniques and color blending.

Intermediate Watercolor Painting with Misuk Goltz

location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
🍷 Barrel Tasting & Tour at Great Bear Vineyards in Davis 🍷
Friday, March 27 | 4:00 PM

title: A Guided Nature Walk (Free event)
description:
Iama Embodied Community
Join Michael for a guided exploration into the practice of slowing down and deepening our relationship with the natural world. This immersive walk invites participants to engage with the subtle intelligence of the landscape, from Blue Oaks and seasonal wildflowers to the presence of scrub jays overhead. Together, we will explore how to “read” trees, interpret the wind, and sit in quiet reflection with grandmother trees. Along the way, we will open conversation about how nature teaches, communicates, and invites us into deeper awareness.

**Location:** Sterling Pointe Ct, Loomis, CA 95650
**Distance:** 3.2 miles
**Elevation Gain:** 375 feet
**Level:** Easy to Moderate
**Pace:** Slow, with frequent stops for observation and discussion
**Meet at trailhead:** 9:30 AM
**Hike Begins:** 9:45 AM
**End Time:** 1:15 PM

Please bring lunch, water, and appropriate footwear.
This experience is designed not as a workout, but as a practice in presence.

### About the guide

Michael is a lifelong hiker, backpacker, and outdoor enthusiast. He is WFR-trained and adult CPR-certified. Michael’s connection to the land has been shaped by journeys through places like Death Valley and the Grand Canyon, though his true roots run deep in Mount Diablo. Guided by curiosity and a love of self-studying trees and plants, he has learned the languages of wind, water, and earth—finding meaning in slowing down and walking with presence.

**P.S.** If you wish to carpool from Sacramento, meet at Parkview Presbyterian Church’s parking lot on 727 T st at 8:30 AM. We will be leaving at 8:45 sharp.

¡Vamos a España—no passport required! Join us for a fun, hands-on Vegan Tapas Cooking Class where bold Spanish flavors meet creative plant-based cooking. Led by the amazing Chef Maggie, founder of V-Karma, this interactive class will have you chopping, blending, tasting, and sipping wine while learning skills you’ll actually use at home. 
✨ Your ticket includes the cooking class + wine

• Savory Spanish olive tapenade with barra de pan
• Spanish-style tomato & butter bean dip
• Cultured cashew cream cheese
• Strawberry cashew cream cheese
• Soft, fluffy tofu ricotta

• Essential knife skills
• How to confidently use blenders & kitchen tools
• Basics of plant-based fermentation
• How to make cultured cashew cheeses & tofu ricotta

location: Davis Musical Theatre Company (DMTC), The Jean Henderson Performing Arts Center, 607 Pena Drive, Davis
description:
Beetlejuice Jr – March 14 through April 5, 2026
School Matinees: March 31, 2026; 9:15am and noon 
  
It’s our show now, kid. The ghost-with-the-most comes to the stage in Beetlejuice JR., the frightfully funny musical based on the beloved motion picture turned Broadway phenomenon. Lydia Deetz is a strange and unusual teenager, still grieving the loss of her mother and obsessed with the whole “being dead thing.” Lucky for Lydia, she and her father move to a new house haunted by a recently deceased couple and Beetlejuice, a delightful demon with a real zest for life. Recommended for ages 10 & up

Choreographed by Logan Sexton
Musical direction by Montana Monce

title: Paired to Perfection: A Six Course Wine and Culinary Experience
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Paired to Perfection: A Six-Course Wine & Culinary Experience in Davis Saturday, March 28 | 6:00–8:30 pm
Get ready for an unforgettable evening where food and wine truly speak the same language.
Join us at Great Bear Vineyards for an elegant, immersive dining experience featuring a six-course, chef-crafted menu, each dish thoughtfully paired with our estate-grown wines. This is more than dinner—it’s a journey through seasonal ingredients, inspired techniques, and wines designed to shine alongside every bite.
Each course is carefully curated to highlight balance, contrast, and harmony between the plate and the glass—showcasing what happens when great food meets great wine 
 Ticket Options:• Dinner Only: $65 + 20% gratuity = $78• Dinner + Paired Wines: $100 + 20% gratuity = $120
 Sample Menu (Inspired by Past Culinary Highlights):• Crisp Fish Tacos — paired with Albariño• Spicy Tuna Tartare — paired with Sauvignon Blanc• Slow-Braised Pork Belly — paired with Chardonnay Frizzante or Tempranillo 2021• Lamb Medallion — paired with Vintner’s Blend 2019• Steak & Mash — paired with Cabernet Sauvignon, Grand Reserve 2020• Olive Oil Cake with Pistachio Icing — paired with our white port-style dessert wine
 Vegetarian guests welcome! A full vegetarian menu is available upon request.
This intimate evening is perfect for food lovers, wine enthusiasts, date nights, or anyone who enjoys savoring life one course at a time. Come experience the essence of Great Bear Vineyards—where every sip and every bite tells a story of craftsmanship, community, and California wine country magic.
 Limited seating. Advance tickets required.

title: Cheese and Wine Pairing
location: Great Bear Vineyards, Great Bear Vineyards 24800 Co Rd 101A, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Wine & Cheese Pairing Experience at Great Bear Vineyards in Davis Sunday, March 29 | 12:00–3:00 pm
If wine and cheese are your love language, this one’s for you.
Join us at Great Bear Vineyards for an afternoon of delicious discovery featuring a curated flight of four award-winning wines, each thoughtfully paired with a premium artisan cheese designed to bring out the very best in both. It’s indulgent, flavorful, and dangerously easy to fall in love with every pairing.
 Want to elevate your experience?Add an exclusive behind-the-scenes barrel room tour, where you’ll see our winemaking process up close and sample estate-grown wines straight from the barrel—a rare and memorable treat.
This experience is perfect whether you’re a devoted foodie, a curious wine lover, or simply in the mood for something delightfully decadent. You’ll get the chance to:• Savor the unique profiles of our handcrafted wines• Discover why certain cheeses make wines shine• Pick up fun, easy pairing tips—or just relax and enjoy every bite and sip
Bring your favorite people and let the flavors unfold. The pairing offers a hearty, satisfying tasting with a focus on flavor harmony. Additional wine and snacks will be available for purchase if you’d like to linger a little longer.
 Tickets required. Space is limited—come sip, snack, and savor with us!

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Healing Arts One-Time Workshop
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
Part of the Pence's Healing Arts Program and run by facilitator Amy Vidra, this one-time workshop focuses more broadly on themes related to depression, anxiety, or major life changes. Through the process of art making, participants will have opportunities for discussion, education, and connecting with peers in a safe and supportive space.

REGISTRATION ONLINE IS REQUIRED IN ADVANCE through the Pence Gallery's website, and space is limited to the first 8 participants who register. Participants must be 18 or older, and no art experience is necessary. This program is generously sponsored by Bob & Joanne Andresen.

Healing Arts One-Time Workshop
Thursday, April 9, 10-11:45 AM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (all materials included)

**Reminder - an RSVP on Meetup does NOT count as registration. Registration is required in advance through the [Pence’s website (CLICK HERE)](https://pencegallery.org/education/programs/workshops-and-classes/).**

\*If you register for this session and are unable to attend, please let us know in advance.

Any questions? Email pencegallery@gmail.com.

title: Art in Action: Woven Fairy House Wall Hangings
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Our fairy houses are back – with a twist! Learn how to make a simple loom with sticks and twine, and weave flowers, leaves, and other natural materials to create your very own wall hanging. Decorate your woven art piece with moss, fabric butterflies, beads, and more to create the perfect fairy hideaway.

Art in Action: Woven Fairy House Wall Hangings
Sunday, April 19, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: Blood on the Clocktower at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
The first Wednesday of every month, we play _Blood on the Clocktower_, a social deduction game in the vein of Werewolf/Mafia/The Resistance, with a few twists!

We'll have a circle of chairs reserved inside Blue Note, near the back, with a Yolo County Board Game Gathering sign on the table. Just say hello, and we'll show you some games, pull some players together, and get to having fun!

We also usually have a few tables of players playing other board games, if social deduction isn't your bag!

Get a feel for Blood on the Clocktower here: https://youtu.be/4sfa8_kNxsQ
source: Meetup Yolo County Board Game Gathering

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.

title: UC Davis Black Family Day
location: UC Davis Quad, 1 Shields Avenue, Davis, CA, 95616
description:
The UC Davis Black Family day is an event to celebrate Black excellence, empowerment, and community. It is open to students, families, and community members. 
  
The event will be hosted on the UC Davis Quad on May 23rd, from Noon to 8 PM. 
  
Attendees will enjoy a day full of entertainers and speakers, honoring the Black community and building conversations around achievements and contemporary issues. Local vendors will be selling delicious food, clothes, books, and much more. Organizations and clubs join the festivities to share information and host engaging activities. 
  
Get ready to enjoy a day full of music, good food, and celebrations of Black culture. The UC Davis Black Family Day is free for all to attend. 
  
For any inquiries, contact: ucdblackfamilyday@ucdavis.edu
